message.content:
Adversary-in-the-Middle: LLMNR or NBT-NS Poisoning and SMB Relay involves adversaries exploiting Microsoft Windows components (LLMNR and NBT-NS) to spoof name resolution services, redirecting network traffic to adversary-controlled systems. This technique allows attackers to collect or relay authentication materials, such as NTLMv1/v2 hashes, which can be cracked offline or relayed to gain unauthorized access and execute code on target systems. Common tools used include Responder, Metasploit, and Impacket modules like ntlmrelayx and smbrelayx. Adversaries like Lazarus Group and Wizard Spider have employed these methods for credential theft and lateral movement.

Potential impacts include unauthorized access, data exfiltration, and further network compromise. Mitigation strategies involve disabling LLMNR and NBT-NS, enforcing strong password policies, using multifactor authentication, and monitoring network traffic for unusual patterns. Additionally, implementing SMB signing and ensuring proper network segmentation can reduce the risk of successful exploitation.
